events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes form build & bracing work at rough-complete stage with "blowout evidence" observed. This is a serious condition - blowout evidence means the formwork has already failed or shows signs of failure (concrete leakage, bulging, displacement of forms). Under ACI 347 (Guide to Formwork for Concrete), formwork must be designed to withstand loads without excessive deflection or failure. Blowout evidence indicates the formwork bracing system is inadequate or has failed.

This is clearly a fail - blowout evidence means the forms have already experienced failure. The condition is "installed-defective" and "failed" since blowout evidence means concrete has escaped or forms have displaced.

Risk is high/critical because blowouts can lead to structural integrity issues, safety hazards, and require immediate corrective action.

Let me construct the JSON response.

At rough-complete stage, any visible blowout means the form assembly is defective and has already experienced structural failure. This is a critical safety and quality non-conformance requiring immediate stop-work and engineering intervention before any further placement proceeds.",
